campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell city council met to review and vote on changes to business license tax rates for the upcoming year. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed tax rate updates, and council members discussed the changes in public before deciding whether to approve the new ordinance.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates being updated for fiscal year
Finance department presented proposed tax changes
City council deliberating and voting on the ordinance
Changes to Campbell Municipal Code

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the standard first reading process to speed things up. The ordinance will be voted on for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading requirement waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the town manager to sign contracts for construction services and a three-year professional services agreement, and began the process for describing planned town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and financial reports
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other construction services
Initiated process to describe and plan town improvements
Approved three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 22, 2026 to organize and prepare for two upcoming council meetings scheduled for April 28 and May 5. They reviewed which items should be added to the agendas, set specific times for discussing certain topics, and checked public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Prepared the draft agenda for the May 5, 2026 meeting
Assigned time slots for specific agenda items to keep meetings on schedule
Reviewed public records and notices from April 9-16, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a study session to review Mountain View's capital improvement plans for the upcoming fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines what major projects and infrastructure improvements the city is planning to fund. They also formally recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week with official proclamations to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Review of planned capital projects and infrastructure spending for 2026-27
Official recognition of Teen Month
Official recognition of National Volunteer Week
Budget allocation discussion for city improvements

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 21, 2026 that included a private closed-door session before the main public meeting. The agenda was relatively brief, focusing on opening remarks and recognizing award winners.
keyTopics: Private closed session (details not disclosed to public)
Award recognition and winners ceremony
Flag salute/opening ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss two legal cases that could expose the city to lawsuits, though specific details weren't shared publicly. The council also recognized staff from the Administrative Services Department for their work managing the city's budget and finances. Residents were given time to speak about any city issues not already on the agenda.
keyTopics: Two pending legal cases the city is monitoring
Recognition of Budget, Finance, and Purchasing staff
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also considered a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs approved for Levi's Stadium area during World Cup 2026
New firefighters union contract negotiated for 2026-2029
Staff withdrew one agenda item before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve several important fiscal and development items for the upcoming year. The council conducted public hearings on tax policies, the 2026-27 development budget, and user fees, while also receiving a presentation on Santa Clara County's plans to address homelessness in the community.
keyTopics: Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
FY 2026-27 development budget approval
Updates to user fees and service charges
County homelessness prevention and response plan
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a decision about removing a protected tree at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at a tree removal permit that was already approved through a quick approval process.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request to have Planning Director review an already-approved decision
Over-the-counter planning approval process being questioned
